name: Hiking from Soria — circular on the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway
name_original: Abejar desde Soria — circular por la Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo
region_intro: Touring cycling around Pinares offers a diverse landscape characterized by extensive pine forests and varied topography. The region features mountainous terrain, scenic waterways, and historic greenways, providing a range of routes. Cyclists can explore areas with significant ecological value, including dense pinyon and stone pine forests. Rivers and reservoirs, such as the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo, contribute to the scenic beauty and offer routes along their banks. Best touring cycling routes around Pinares The most popular touring cycling route is Hiking from Soria — circular on the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway, a 37.8 miles (60.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a historic greenway, offering a smooth and scenic ride. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Tour around the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo — circular from Molinos de Duero, a moderate 23.0 miles (37.1 km) path. This route circles a large reservoir, providing expansive water views and forested sections. Local touring cyclists also love the La Junta de los Rios -- circular from Soria, a 13.4 miles (21.6 km) trail leading through river confluence areas, often completed in about 1 hour 31 minutes. Touring cycling around Pinares is defined by extensive pine forests, scenic rivers, and historic greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to challenging climbs. The routes in Pinares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 1,600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Pinares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Pinares?
A: Pinares offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 170 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy paths to challenging climbs through diverse landscapes.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Pinares?
A: The touring cycling routes in Pinares offer a good mix of difficulties. You'll find 15 easy routes perfect for a relaxed ride, 64 moderate routes for those seeking a bit more challenge, and 94 difficult routes for experienced cyclists looking for significant elevation gains and longer distances.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Pinares?
A: Yes, Pinares has several routes ideal for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042251034/la-junta-de-los-rios-circular-from-soria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Junta de los Rios -- circular from Soria&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 13.4-mile (21.6 km) trail that leads through scenic river confluence areas, perfect for a gentle introduction to touring cycling in the region.

Q: What options are there for experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge in Pinares?
A: Experienced touring cyclists will find plenty of challenging routes in Pinares, with 94 difficult options available. A demanding choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036846563/hontoria-del-pinar-from-abejar-circular-via-the-santander-mediterranean-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hontoria del Pinar from Abejar — circular via the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, a 44.5-mile (71.6 km) route with significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while touring cycling in Pinares?
A: Pinares is defined by its extensive pine forests, which provide shaded and scenic routes. You'll also encounter varied topography, including mountainous terrain, picturesque rivers like the Lozoya River, and large reservoirs such as the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo. Some areas feature unique geological formations and historic greenways.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Pinares?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Pinares are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041722151/tour-around-the-embalse-de-la-cuerda-del-pozo-circular-from-molinos-de-duero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo — circular from Molinos de Duero&lt;/a&gt;, which provides expansive water views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041649794/embalse-de-la-cuerda-del-pozo-circular-desde-soria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo — circular desde Soria&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that follow historic greenways in Pinares?
A: Yes, Pinares features routes that utilize historic greenways, offering smooth and scenic rides. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036830986/hiking-from-soria-circular-on-the-santander-mediterranean-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking from Soria — circular on the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, a 37.8-mile (60.8 km) moderate trail that follows a historic railway line.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Pinares?
A: The touring cycling routes in Pinares are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse terrain, the beauty of the pine forests, and the well-maintained paths, especially those along greenways and reservoirs.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the touring cycling routes in Pinares?
A: Yes, Pinares offers several interesting landmarks and natural attractions. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2516913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión&lt;/a&gt;, visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3402819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Saturio&lt;/a&gt;, or see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3328794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Duero River&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features the charming town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2721568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vinuesa and the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2721569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Submerged Roman Bridge of Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Pinares?
A: Pinares generally enjoys a mild climate for much of the year, making it suitable for touring cycling across extended seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer can be enjoyable, especially on routes that pass through shaded pine forests or along reservoirs. Winter cycling is also an option, though conditions may vary.

Q: Are there any routes that explore natural parks or protected areas?
A: Yes, you can find routes that take you through or near significant natural areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036806150/hiking-route-from-san-leonardo-de-yaguee-canon-del-rio-lobos-natural-park-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking route from San Leonardo de Yagüe — Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 25.8-mile (41.6 km) route that allows you to experience the natural beauty of the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The region's reservoirs are a major scenic highlight. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041722151/tour-around-the-embalse-de-la-cuerda-del-pozo-circular-from-molinos-de-duero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo — circular from Molinos de Duero&lt;/a&gt; provide expansive water views and opportunities to cycle along their banks.
